---
title: "Archive"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-07"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is a boneyard for old unused code that seemed too handy to delete.

# Specialized superfunction: Used for single sample sizes

This superfunction variant was made to handle single mixtures, as the
process is different enough from multiple mixtures to warrant it's own
function.

```{r singular superfunction}
simulate_complete_single <- function() {
  grid <- expand.grid(
  n          = seq(100, 1000, by = 200), # We go from 100 to 1000 sample size, in iterations of 200
  kappa_mean = seq(10, 100, by = 20), # We go from 10 to 100 sample size, in iterations of 20
  rep        = 1:25 # 25 repetitions to do Monte Carlo simulation
) %>% as_tibble()
  
  K <- 1 # Only a single mixture
  d <- 3 # Three dimensions, as per usual


with_progress({
  p <- progressor(steps = nrow(grid)) # Want to have progress bar if this is being ran on it's own. Otherwise, we have no idea where the simulation is going, or if it is actually running. Crucial because it rarely, but sometimes, crashes.
  
  results <- future_lapply(seq_len(nrow(grid)), function(i) {
    p(sprintf("Sim %d", i))
    row <- grid[i, ]
    
    mu_list <- list(c(1, 0, 0))
    kappas  <- make_kappas(K, row$kappa_mean, variation = 1)
    
    # Generate data from a single vMF component
    sim <- simulate_vmf_mixture(
      d          = d,
      K_true     = K,
      n          = row$n,
      w          = 1,                    
      mu_list    = mu_list,
      k_list     = kappas,
      force_min_one = TRUE
    )
    
    vmf_fit <- tryCatch(
      movMF::movMF(sim$X, k = K, nruns = 3),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    vmf_cluster <- if (is.null(vmf_fit)) NA else predict(vmf_fit)
    vmf_score   <- if (all(is.na(vmf_cluster))) NA else   within_cosine(sim$X, vmf_cluster)
    
    km       <- kmeans(sim$X, centers = K, nstart = 5)
    km_score <- within_cosine(sim$X, km$cluster)
    
    data.frame(
      n          = row$n,
      kappa_mean = row$kappa_mean,
      rep        = row$rep,
      vmf_score  = vmf_score,
      km_score   = km_score
    )
  }, future.seed = TRUE)
  
  results <- bind_rows(results)
})

win_df <- results %>%
  group_by(n, kappa_mean) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_tightness = mean(vmf_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

invisible(list(raw = results, summary = win_df))
}
```
